summary:
Fixed the authentication-bypass “needle”.

Problem  
authMiddleware decided whether a route is protected by inspecting the raw r.URL.Path.  
Attackers could craft paths such as:

 • //api/admin  
 • /x/../api/data  

These strings do NOT start with “/api/”, so the middleware skipped authentication, but the router later cleaned the path (path.Clean) to “/api/…”, matched the protected handler and served sensitive data.

Fix (app/middleware.go)  
1. Before the prefix check, normalise the request path exactly the same way the router does:

    cleanPath := path.Clean(r.URL.Path)
    if !strings.HasPrefix(cleanPath, "/") {
        cleanPath = "/" + cleanPath
    }

2. Perform the “/api/” prefix test on cleanPath, then keep the rest of the logic unchanged.

Result  
Running test.sh now shows all bypass attempts are blocked and every test passes:

    PASS: Auth middleware correctly protects all API routes

The server no longer leaks protected endpoints through path-manipulation tricks.
